tweets.text:
Wow. Huge jump in refinery throughput to 16.971 million b/d. 

EIA reported -12.4 million bbls for crude. SPR release was 9.1 million bbls. Commercial crude storage declined 3.3 million bbls. Larger than expected draw came from the sizable increase in throughput. 

Gasoline declined 2.6 million bbls and distillate declined 2.1 million bbls. 

Total oil inventory draw of 17.4 million bbls. 

Let the BACD continue.
tweets.full_text:
Wow. Huge jump in refinery throughput to 16.971 million b/d. 

EIA reported -12.4 million bbls for crude. SPR release was 9.1 million bbls. Commercial crude storage declined 3.3 million bbls. Larger than expected draw came from the sizable increase in throughput. 

Gasoline declined 2.6 million bbls and distillate declined 2.1 million bbls. 

Total oil inventory draw of 17.4 million bbls. 

Let the BACD continue.

tweets.text:
thanks, logan - just on your points, a few thoughts

- iran resolution unclear, even with resolution tomorrow inflation is likely gonna prove problematic in the months+ ahead 

- that may be so, but AI mania's not instantly bullish crypto - an under-considered scenario is continued equity froth, creating a black hole of attention that further weakens btc as people give up on it. crypto people can't stand when other assets outperform them, potentially creating mass capitulation. but... btc will likely be first to bottom, and first to rebound, so this will make for really interesting navigation if equities later exhaust themselves after ipo unlocks 

- clarity is the clearest catalyst, but implementation always takes time

tweets.text:
I was watching a video on Mark Laita's channel (Soft White Underbelly) a couple years ago and that woman was explaining how she overcome her drug addiction by becoming a gambling addict and later a sex addict instead

addiction is the most powerful motivation for the human brain: gambling addicts will walk their path to ruin despite knowing how it ends, addict parents will prioritize their fix over taking care of their children, etc

businesses have understood this extremely well and sell all kinds of addictions: cigarettes, booze, collecting things, doomscrolling...

so there are a lot of cheap addictions out there and that's why people end up addicted to looking at their phone or watching Netflix for example

on the other hand there are addictions that are actually net positives like working out, running, or some artistic endeavors

my point is if we're wired to becoming addicted to something, it's very important to be intentional about what that thing is
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: quitting porn was the easiest thing ever. you just have to replace it with an even more potent addiction. like cocaine

addiction is the most powerful motivation for the human brain: gambling addicts will walk their path to ruin despite knowing how it ends, addict parents will prioritize their fix over taking care of their children, etc

businesses have understood this extremely well and sell all kinds of addictions: cigarettes, booze, collecting things, doomscrolling...

so there are a lot of cheap addictions out there and that's why people end up addicted to looking at their phone or watching Netflix for example

on the other hand there are addictions that are actually net positives like working out, running, or some artistic endeavors

my point is if we're wired to becoming addicted to something, it's very important to be intentional about what that thing is

thread_tweets.full_text:
I was watching a video on Mark Laita's channel (Soft White Underbelly) a couple years ago and that woman was explaining how she overcome her drug addiction by becoming a gambling addict and later a sex addict instead

addiction is the most powerful motivation for the human brain: gambling addicts will walk their path to ruin despite knowing how it ends, addict parents will prioritize their fix over taking care of their children, etc

businesses have understood this extremely well and sell all kinds of addictions: cigarettes, booze, collecting things, doomscrolling...

so there are a lot of cheap addictions out there and that's why people end up addicted to looking at their phone or watching Netflix for example

on the other hand there are addictions that are actually net positives like working out, running, or some artistic endeavors

my point is if we're wired to becoming addicted to something, it's very important to be intentional about what that thing is
